raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I bought shoes on a scam website called XXXX. As soon as I submitted my credit card information I knew it was a scam because of the sketchy email. The charge that showed on my CC statement was " XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  {$180.00}. I submitted a dispute for that as it was a scam. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, the scammers attempted many many purchases through XXXX XXXX XXXX  a few of the purchases were declined, but two purchases went through : - XXXX  : {$2700.00} -XXXX : {$13000.00} The AMEX Fraud team called me themselves due to the suspicious activity. I said it was 100 % not me that made the purchases and it was fraud. They closed my credit card and sent me a new one and credited my account for the amount. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, the AMEX Fraud team called me and asked if I put my credit card information to the scam website I said that I did. They then rejected my Fraud Claim on the terms that I gave the website my information, and debited my account {$16000.00}. I 100000 % never made these purchases.
